question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Liquidation bypass or incorrect liquidation that lets unhealthy borrowers avoid liquidation or lets liquidators seize excess collateral'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit with fee-on-transfer loan token] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploit a fee-on-transfer loan token where permit succeeds but safeTransferFrom delivers less than amount under PRECONDITIONS where loanToken charges a transfer fee, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) repayAndWithdrawCollateral called with fee-on-transfer loanToken, (2) pullToken calls permit then safeTransferFrom(token, from, bundler, amount), (3) bundler receives amount - fee, (4) forceApproveMax approves Midnight for type(uint256).max, (5) repay(market, units, onBehalf) where units = amount - referralFee but bundler only has amount - fee, (6) Midnight pulls units from bundler but bundler has insufficient balance, (7) revert blocks repayment, violating INVARIANT that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: repay-based liquidation bypass due to fee-on-transfer token shortfall?
